audienceSections.normal: This chapter teaches [[grammar:clause + waardoor + clause]] and [[grammar:number + meter/centimeter/gram]]. Use [[grammar:waardoor]] for as a result of which. Put the number before the unit: twee [[grammar:meter]], vijftig [[grammar:centimeter]].
audienceSections.expert: This chapter introduces [[grammar:clause + waardoor + clause]]: [[grammar:Waardoor]] introduces a relative-result clause that links the consequence to the preceding event.; [[grammar:number + meter/centimeter/gram]]: Exact metric measurements use a numeral followed by the unit noun; abbreviations are deferred to later practice.

audienceSections.normal:
#### clause + waardoor + clause

Use [[grammar:waardoor]] for as a result of which. Example: [[grammar:De kast is tien centimeter te breed, waardoor de deur niet sluit.]]

#### number + meter/centimeter/gram

Put the number before the unit: twee [[grammar:meter]], vijftig [[grammar:centimeter]]. Example: [[grammar:Milan meet een plank van twee meter.]]
audienceSections.expert:
#### clause + waardoor + clause

[[grammar:Waardoor]] introduces a relative-result clause that links the consequence to the preceding event. Literal example: [[grammar:De kast is tien centimeter te breed, waardoor de deur niet sluit.]]

#### number + meter/centimeter/gram

Exact metric measurements use a numeral followed by the unit noun; abbreviations are deferred to later practice. Literal example: [[grammar:Milan meet een plank van twee meter.]]

audienceSections.normal: [[grammar:Waardoor]] presents a result. Dutch measurement phrases put the number before the metric unit.
audienceSections.expert: [[grammar:Waardoor]] presents a result. Dutch measurement phrases put the number before the metric unit.

breakdown.normal:
- [[grammar:Mark meet de nieuwe kamer met een meetlint.]] — Mark measures the new room with a tape measure.
- [[grammar:De lange muur is vijf meter lang.]] — The long wall is five meters long.
- [[grammar:De korte muur is drie meter breed.]] — The short wall is three meters wide.
- [[grammar:Milan meet een plank van twee meter.]] — Milan measures a shelf of two meters.
- [[grammar:De kast is tien centimeter te breed, waardoor de deur niet sluit.]] — The cupboard is ten centimeters too wide, as a result of which the door does not close.
- [[grammar:Pieter verplaatst de kast vijftig centimeter naar links.]] — Pieter moves the cupboard fifty centimeters to the left.
- [[grammar:Een kleine doos weegt negentig gram.]] — A small box weighs ninety grams.
- [[grammar:De grote doos is zwaar, waardoor Milan hulp vraagt.]] — The large box is heavy, so Milan asks for help.
- [[grammar:Mark meet elke muur nog een keer.]] — Mark measures every wall one more time.
- [[grammar:Daarna passen de planken precies in de kamer.]] — Afterward the shelves fit exactly in the room.

- [[grammar:De kast is tien centimeter te breed, waardoor de deur niet sluit.]] — Literal reading evidence at Narrative > paragraph 2 > sentence 1.
- [[grammar:Pieter verplaatst de kast vijftig centimeter naar links.]] — Literal reading evidence at Narrative > paragraph 2 > sentence 2.
- [[grammar:Een kleine doos weegt negentig gram.]] — Literal reading evidence at Narrative > paragraph 2 > sentence 3.
- [[grammar:De grote doos is zwaar, waardoor Milan hulp vraagt.]] — Literal reading evidence at Narrative > paragraph 3 > sentence 1.
- [[grammar:Mark meet elke muur nog een keer.]] — Literal reading evidence at Narrative > paragraph 3 > sentence 2.
